raw_recent_text: "Ugh, not again," you spout as the old wagon shutters, then dies.
Stranded on the road again, but this time, your money has gone dry.
All but your 50 dollar bill that Grandma gave you on her last Christmas.
You've been saving it for when you needed it most, but surely, it won't be enough.
The door creaks open, and you step out into the night sky, coughing up the smoke from your fried vehicle.
After pushing your car off the road and between the trees, there isn't much else left for you to do,
so you begin to wander down the dark, lonely street.
But at the end of the road, where concrete turned to stone turned to dirt, you notice a light up ahead, on the top of a hill.
As you waltz into the old, wooden shack, your eyes begin to light up with the fire of a thousand suns.
Roulette wheels! Poker tables! And in a dark corner of the abandoned casino, sits a dealer, shuffling cards for a new round of Blackjack.
That 50 dollars might just come in handy after all. Thanks, Grandma!
As you go to sit down at the table, you hear the Dealer cough, then watch as he sits up.
In a deep, and yet strained voice, the Dealer, cloaked in darkness, poses a question to you.
Would you like to play a game of Blackjack?
You have $50

"Remember, it's not paranoia if your plants are actually plotting against you. Keep them in check by pretending to water them with vinegar."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 2 ~ ~ ~  True
You wake up and actually want to get out of the car. This is a historic moment.
Your car breaks down on a stretch of road with no cell service.
The sun beats down. You're stuck.
You sit on the hood and wait. And wait. And wait.
Three hours later, someone finally stops.
They charge you $25 for the ride. At least you're not dead.
Your new balance is $48 - $25 = $23
The itching is maddening. Especially at night. Tiny burrows appearing between your fingers, on your wrists, in your armpits.
Microscopic mites have burrowed into your skin. They're laying eggs under your flesh.
You scratch until you bleed, but the relief is only momentary.
Scabies. Your skin is infested.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
The green felt remembers you. So do I.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
Night falls. The game begins.

available_routes: No effort. No suspense. Just the house being the house.

raw_recent_text: Your efforts haven't gone unnoticed. They're just not always mentioned.
Here's what my cat whispered to me last night:
"If you give up on your dreams, that may free up some time to get some actual stuff done"
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 3 ~ ~ ~  True
You wake up and actually want to get out of the car. This is a historic moment.
Your heart is pounding out of your chest. You can't breathe. You're dying - you're sure of it.
Except you're not. This is a panic attack. But it feels like death.
Trembling, sweating, derealization. The world doesn't feel real.
Severe anxiety disorder. You need medication. Therapy. Something.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
Welcome, welcome. Have a seat, and we can begin.
Sleep won't come. You try counting sheep. The sheep turn into cards. The cards turn into debts. The debts turn into a spiral.
You lie there, eyes wide open, listening to your own breathing and the distant hum of the highway.
Somewhere out there, normal people are sleeping in beds. In houses. With futures.
You wonder if they know how lucky they are. Probably not. Nobody does until it's gone.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You keep coming back. Interesting.
You have $21

Yippee!
You've survived 3 days!
Yesterday, at this time, you had $21.
Since then, you've managed to lose $2.
That brings you to a grand total of $19!
You're in the 'ramen noodle' tax bracket.
You've got a unique path, and honestly, it's thrilling to watch it unfold.
I read this once in a magazine:
"You may have breathed the same air a dinosaur breathed 1000s of years ago. If you don't think that's the tightest shit then get out of my face."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 4 ~ ~ ~  True
You stretch out and feel every vertebra in your spine crack in the most satisfying way. Today is going to be good.
Your steering feels off. You get out and check - one tire is low. Not flat, but definitely losing air.
You have to keep stopping to put air in. Every gas station. Every hour.
Your afternoon becomes a series of air pump visits.
You head straight to the casino, hoping for better luck at the tables than you had with your car.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
Shuffle. Deal. Win. Lose. Repeat. Shall we?

you head straight to the casino, hoping for better luck at the tables than you had with your car.
as the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more blackjack.
shuffle. deal. win. lose. repeat. shall we?

the dealer's next card is a ten of diamonds

over 21. the dealer exhales through his nose like a dragon who just lost a bet. oh wait.

your new balance is $24 + $2 = $26
after playing a few rounds of blackjack, the dealer points to the door.
without questioning his word, and with your winnings in hand, you scurry to the door, eager to get some sleep.
making it back to your car, ditched on the side of the road, you lay down, and close your eyes. it's time to rest.

raw_recent_text: Congrats!
You've survived 4 days!
Yesterday, at this time, you had $19.
Since then, you've accumulated $7.
That brings you to a grand total of $26!
Poor? Yes. Defeated? Not yet.
Life's a garden, dig it. You're doing just that, and it's admirable.
If you ever feel lost, just remember:
"If at first you don't succeed, redefine success. Because if success is waking up at noon on a Wednesday thinking it's a Saturday, then congratulations, you've made it."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 5 ~ ~ ~  True
You wake up smiling. When's the last time that happened? You can't remember, but it happened today.
You drive your car to a gas station for supplies. Just snacks. Maybe some coffee.
You're browsing the chips when the door slams open.
"EVERYBODY ON THE GROUND! NOW!"
A man with a shotgun. Ski mask. Shaking hands. This is really happening.
The cashier freezes. A mother clutches her child. An old man drops his coffee.
The robber swings the gun around, wild-eyed.
You drive your car to a gas station for supplies. Just snacks. Maybe some coffee.
You're browsing the chips when the door slams open.
"EVERYBODY ON THE GROUND! NOW!"
A man with a shotgun. Ski mask. Shaking hands. This is really happening.
The cashier freezes. A mother clutches her child. An old man drops his coffee.
The robber swings the gun around, wild-eyed.
You duck behind a shelf. Slowly, carefully, you crawl toward the back.
The robber is focused on the register. You're almost to the storage room...
You make it. Hide behind boxes of toilet paper and canned goods.
You can hear everything. The shouting. The crying. The crash of the register hitting the floor.
Then sirens. Running footsteps. Silence.
The police find you an hour later, still hiding. Curled up like a child.
Your mind feels... foggy. Something isn't right.
For once, you sleep peacefully. No dreams. No nightmares. Just rest.
You wake up feeling... good? Is this what normal people feel like?
It won't last, but you appreciate it while it does.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Each night you return. Each night, the cards wait.

Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 6 ~ ~ ~  True
Your current sanity: 75%
Your body feels like it got a factory reset overnight. Joints? Smooth. Back? Fine. Mood? Immaculate.
You can't remember if you slept last night. Or the night before.
You weren't paying attention. The needle went right into your hand.
It wasn't clean. Rusty. Used. You don't know where it came from.
Blood is beading at the puncture site. Your heart is racing with dread.
What was on that needle? Hepatitis? HIV? Tetanus?
You need to get to a doctor. Get tested. Get prophylaxis. NOW.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Still alive? Good. The cards would be disappointed otherwise.
The sky is obscene tonight. Purple-black and crusted with stars. More than you've ever seen.
It's the kind of sky city people never see because there's always a Walgreens sign drowning it out.
But out here, parked behind a gas station at the edge of nowhere? You get the whole show.
Still, it's beautiful. You feel small, but in a comforting way.

That brings you to a grand total of $40!
You're basically living paycheck to paycheck. Except there's no paycheck.
For what it's worth, I think you're doing alright.
Something to ponder on your journey:
"I walk around like everything's fine, but deep down, inside my shoe, my sock is sliding off."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 7 ~ ~ ~  True
You had a dream about winning at Blackjack. Then you woke up. But you still feel great.
You're looking for a dropped quarter under the driver's seat when your hand
touches something cold and hard. Metal.
You pull it out. It's a lockbox. A real, proper lockbox. Steel. Heavy. About the size
of a shoebox. There's a keyhole on the front and a combination dial on top.
It's padlocked shut with a serious-looking Master lock.
You shake it. Something slides around inside. Multiple somethings.
They clink against each other like glass or metal.
This wasn't here yesterday. You'd have noticed. Which means someone put it
in your car while you were sleeping.
On the bottom, scratched into the metal with something sharp:
"DON'T OPEN UNTIL READY"
Probably wise. You don't know what's in there. Could be valuables.
Could be evidence. Could be somebody's ashes.
Could be a lot of things.
You put the lockbox in your trunk. You need to find whoever left it —
or at least find the key.
You taste copper. There's nothing in your mouth.
In the nightmare, the Dealer has your face. He deals with your hands. He smiles with your mouth.
You sit across from yourself and lose every hand. You can't beat yourself. You never could.
You wake up and avoid the mirror for the rest of the morning.
Your mind feels... foggy. Something isn't right.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
Come, sit down, we have a game to play.

Your current sanity: 69%
You had a dream about winning at Blackjack. Then you woke up. But you still feel great.
The oysters tasted... off. You ate them anyway.
Big mistake.
Within hours, you're violently ill. Vomiting, diarrhea, fever, chills.
Shellfish poisoning. Vibrio bacteria. Could be fatal without treatment.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
The table is set. The cards are ready. Are you?
Midnight. You lace up your shoes and walk into the dark.
A cat crosses your path. It doesn't run. Just sits and watches you, like it's been expecting you.
You nod at the cat. It blinks. You keep walking. Some conversations don't need words.
You walk three miles in a loop. Past the casino, past the gas station, past the diner, back to the wagon. A familiar orbit.
When you get back to your car, you feel calmer. Tired in the right way.

You look... determined. Or desperate. Hard to tell.
